rationale: Rejects compiled-binary delivery targets for the C1 playable parity product so no downstream step drifts toward a packaged `.exe`, installer, wrapper binary, or bundled redistributable. The required runtime target remains exactly `bun run doom.ts` (D-FPS-003, `plan_fps/manifests/00-003-pin-bun-run-doom-entrypoint.json`). Locks the rejected target kinds, forbidden artifact file extensions, forbidden build commands, forbidden `package.json` script names, and the current-workspace posture: `package.json` only wires `format` and `start` scripts, `tsconfig.json` sets `noEmit: true`, and no forbidden artifact exists at the workspace root today.

rejectedTargets.description: A standalone native executable produced by compiling or packaging TypeScript source into a platform binary (for example `doom.exe`).
A native executable whose only job is to launch a Bun runtime or TypeScript source at start-up (for example a Windows `.exe` that shells out to `bun run`).
An installer or setup executable that deploys or registers the playable product on the host machine (for example an `.msi` or `setup.exe`).
A bundled, packed, or standalone redistributable produced by `bun build --compile`, `pkg`, `nexe`, or equivalent tooling.

readmeRejectionLine: Final target is not a compiled executable, wrapper executable, installer, or packaged binary.
